title: API Pagination Redesign
description: Your team needs to add pagination to a REST API that currently returns all records at once. Four specialists are ready. Your decisions shape who leads, who stays silent, and what the team builds.

smarter.label: Smarter
smarter.description: Local model with thinking -- free inference, your GPU

smartest.label: Smartest
smartest.description: Local reasoning distilled to Claude for polished output

comparison.label: Without Cohort
comparison.description: Every agent responds to every message (no gating)

context_sources.persona: System design specialist with 12 learned facts about REST patterns
context_sources.memory: 3 prior conversations about this API's scaling issues

context_sources.persona: Python specialist with 18 learned facts about FastAPI + SQLAlchemy patterns
context_sources.memory: 5 prior sessions -- knows existing codebase patterns, test conventions
context_sources.grounding: Current repo structure + existing endpoint source code

context_sources.persona: QA specialist with 9 learned facts about edge case patterns
context_sources.memory: 2 prior sessions -- knows existing test suite coverage gaps
context_sources.grounding: Test coverage report + CI pipeline configuration

context_sources.persona: Research specialist with 14 learned facts about API design precedents
context_sources.memory: 4 prior sessions -- catalogued patterns from 20+ production APIs
context_sources.grounding: Document library with 8 curated articles on pagination strategies

phases.name: DISCOVER
phases.description: Researching the existing API and understanding the problem space.

steps.text: The architect opens the discussion. Cohort's scoring engine evaluates all four agents to decide who's most relevant.
Let's investigate the existing /users endpoint before we redesign it. We need to research what similar APIs do for pagination.

steps.title: Who should respond?
steps.explanation: The message contains 'investigate', 'research', 'existing' -- all DISCOVER-phase keywords that match the Researcher's triggers.

scores.reason: 3 trigger matches (research, investigate, existing) + DISCOVER phase alignment
Domain owner (api, endpoint) but just spoke -- novelty penalty
Low trigger match, waiting for implementation phase
No trigger matches. Observer threshold is 0.80 -- gated.

steps.insight: The Tester is gated as OBSERVER. Score 0.22 is well below the 0.80 threshold needed for observers to speak. This prevents the tester from jumping in prematurely.
steps.cost_note: Without Cohort, all 4 agents would respond here. With Cohort, only the Researcher is recommended. That's 3 unnecessary responses avoided.

steps.text: Investigated 5 similar REST APIs. All use cursor-based pagination for large datasets. The existing endpoint returns max 100 records with no pagination at all -- that's the gap.
Good research. Now the team needs to decide on an approach. This is where YOUR choice changes the conversation.

phases.prompt: The architect asks for your input on the migration strategy. What matters more to your team?

options.label: Backward Compatibility
options.description: Keep existing callers working. Pagination is opt-in. No breaking changes.
options.consequence_preview: Conservative approach -- more research, more constraints on the developer.

options.label: Clean Break
options.description: Redesign the endpoint properly. Existing callers migrate to v2.
options.consequence_preview: Aggressive approach -- developer gets the green light faster.

steps.text: You chose backward compatibility. Watch how this shifts the conversation -- the Researcher stays relevant longer because migration risk needs investigation.

OK, backward compatibility is the priority. We need to design this so existing callers that don't pass a cursor parameter get the current behavior. The pagination envelope wraps around the existing response format.

Important finding: we have 14 internal services and 3 external partners calling /users. Two partners have strict response schema validation. Any envelope change will break them unless we version the endpoint.

meta.smartest_note: Escalated to Smartest -- constraint analysis benefits from Claude's reasoning on dependency impact.

steps.title: Constraint discovery changes the scores
steps.explanation: The Researcher's finding introduces real constraints. The Developer can't just build -- they need to navigate compatibility requirements.

scores.reason: High-value constraint discovery. Novelty remains high because each finding is new information.
Design authority on versioning strategy. 'design', 'endpoint' triggers active.
Barely above 0.30 threshold. Can speak but isn't the priority.
Still waiting. Validation phase hasn't started.

steps.insight: Compare this to the Clean Break branch: there, the Developer scores 0.54 and the Researcher drops to 0.29. Your choice kept the Researcher relevant and constrained the Developer.
steps.cost_note: Tester still gated. Developer can speak but Cohort ranked them last. In a naive system, all 4 would respond -- that's 2 wasted responses with ~3K tokens each.

steps.text: Two-phase approach: Phase 1 adds ?cursor support but keeps the flat array response for callers without cursor. Phase 2 migrates to envelope format with a 90-day deprecation window. @developer the implementation needs a response format switch based on whether cursor is present.

meta.smartest_note: Escalated to Smartest -- migration design benefits from Claude synthesizing backward-compat constraints with the Researcher's findings.

steps.text: Understood. That means two serialization paths in the endpoint. I'll implement a response adapter that detects legacy vs. paginated mode. More complex but doable.

steps.title: Developer's complexity acknowledgment
steps.explanation: The developer accepted a harder implementation. 'implement', 'code' keywords shift toward EXECUTE phase.

scores.reason: EXECUTE keywords + direct involvement. Rising.
Still relevant but dropping -- design decisions are settling.
Moved to APPROVED_SILENT. Constraints delivered. Threshold now 0.70 -- score too low to keep talking.
Still gated. Approaching but not yet.

steps.insight: The Researcher gets APPROVED_SILENT status -- they contributed valuable constraints and Cohort recognized it. They won't talk again unless something changes.
steps.cost_note: Researcher gated after contributing 2 high-value messages. Without Cohort, they'd keep talking -- diminishing returns with increasing cost.

steps.reason: Contributed 2 high-value messages. Novelty exhausted for current topic.

steps.text: You chose a clean break. Watch how this ACCELERATES the Developer -- less constraint research needed, faster path to implementation.
Clean break it is. We'll create /v2/users with cursor-based pagination as the default. The v1 endpoint stays frozen -- no changes, just a deprecation header. Clean contract from day one.

steps.title: Clean break shifts power to the Developer
steps.explanation: With no backward-compat constraints, the conversation moves straight to implementation. 'create', 'build' are EXECUTE-phase keywords.

scores.reason: 'create' + 'build' trigger EXECUTE phase. Developer's domain. Fast-tracked.
Design authority on the v2 contract. Still active.
No more research needed. Clean break means fewer unknowns. Threshold 0.70.
Still waiting for something to test.

steps.insight: The Researcher is already silenced! In the Compat branch, they're still actively contributing constraints at this point. Your choice gave the Developer a 2-message head start.
steps.cost_note: 2 agents gated immediately. Without Cohort, you'd be paying for Researcher and Tester responses that add nothing to a clean-break discussion.

steps.reason: No constraint research needed for clean-break approach.

steps.text: Nice, clean slate. I'll implement cursor-based pagination with: opaque cursor tokens, configurable page size (default 25, max 100), and a standard envelope: { data: [], next_cursor: string|null, has_more: boolean }.
Good structure. Add a total_count field to the envelope -- clients need it for progress indicators. And the cursor should encode both the sort field and direction so it's self-describing.
Total count is expensive on large tables -- it forces a COUNT(*) on every request. I'll add it as an optional parameter: ?include=total_count. Only computed when explicitly requested.

meta.smartest_note: Escalated to Smartest -- pushback on architect's suggestion benefits from Claude's nuanced reasoning about database performance trade-offs.

steps.title: Productive disagreement
steps.explanation: Developer pushed back on architect's suggestion with a valid technical reason. Both agents remain ACTIVE because this is high-value exchange.

scores.reason: Leading the implementation discussion. High novelty -- each message adds new technical detail.
Still contributing design refinements but developer is leading.
Promoted from OBSERVER! 'validation', 'test' keywords emerging in context.
Moved to DORMANT. Fully disengaged from this clean-break discussion.

steps.insight: In the Compat branch, the Researcher is still scoring 0.33 here. In Clean Break, they've dropped to DORMANT at 0.22. The Developer has had 2 extra productive exchanges. Same agents, completely different dynamics.

phases.name: EXECUTE
phases.description: Implementation underway. The developer is building.

steps.text: Implementation is underway. The developer builds the pagination module. Now something unexpected happens during testing...
Implementation complete. The module handles forward/backward cursors, empty result sets, and invalid cursor tokens with proper HTTP 400 error codes.

steps.title: Developer's novelty is dropping
steps.explanation: The developer has spoken multiple times. Cohort's novelty scoring penalizes repetition -- even good contributions lose score after 2-3 messages.

scores.reason: 'test', 'validation', 'edge' keywords. Tester's time is coming.
Novelty penalty after 2+ messages. Score dropping despite owning the implementation.
Review authority. Moderate relevance.
Deeply dormant. Threshold 1.00 -- effectively locked out.

steps.insight: This is Cohort's loop prevention. Without scoring, the developer would keep talking about implementation details. The novelty penalty naturally creates space for the tester.
steps.cost_note: Researcher has been gated for multiple rounds. Each round saves ~3.5K tokens of unnecessary inference.

steps.text: Starting validation. Edge cases: empty result sets, expired cursors, concurrent modifications during pagination, and load test with 10k records. Also running regression against existing callers.
During testing, the tester discovers something. Your second choice determines what they find -- and this changes which agents re-engage.

phases.prompt: The tester runs comprehensive validation. What do they discover?

options.label: Security Vulnerability
options.description: The cursor token is a base64-encoded database ID -- information disclosure risk.
options.consequence_preview: Watch a DORMANT agent wake up when the topic shifts.

options.label: Performance Cliff
options.description: Pagination works fine until page 400+, then response times spike to 12 seconds.
options.consequence_preview: The developer stays hot -- this is an implementation problem.

phases.name: TOPIC SHIFT
phases.description: A security vulnerability shifts the conversation back to research mode.

steps.text: Security finding. Watch what happens to the DORMANT Researcher...

CRITICAL: The cursor token is a base64-encoded database row ID. Anyone can decode it, enumerate records, and infer data volume. This is an information disclosure vulnerability. We need to investigate encryption options and research how other APIs handle this.

meta.smartest_note: Escalated to Smartest -- security vulnerability analysis benefits from Claude's broader knowledge of information disclosure patterns.

steps.title: Topic shift re-engages the Researcher!
steps.explanation: 'investigate', 'research', 'similar' -- the security finding pulled the conversation back to DISCOVER-phase language. The Researcher was DORMANT (threshold 1.00) but topic shift triggers a full re-evaluation.

scores.reason: RE-ENGAGED from DORMANT! Topic shift detected: 'investigate' + 'research' match triggers. Status reset to ACTIVE.
Owns this finding. High ownership score.
Security architecture implications. Re-engaged.
Will need to fix this. Implementation expertise relevant.

steps.insight: This is Cohort's topic shift detection in action. The Researcher went from DORMANT (score 0.18, threshold 1.00 -- effectively locked out) to ACTIVE (score 0.61) in a single message. The system recognized that 'investigate encryption' and 'research how other APIs handle this' are the Researcher's exact domain.
steps.cost_note: The Researcher was gated for 4+ rounds, saving tokens each time. Now they're re-engaged because they're genuinely needed. Cohort doesn't just cut agents -- it brings them back at the right moment.

steps.reason: Topic shift to research/investigation domain. Status: DORMANT -> ACTIVE.

steps.text: Good catch. Researched cursor encryption in similar APIs. Best practice is HMAC-signed opaque tokens. Three options: itsdangerous (lightweight, Flask ecosystem), PyJWT (standard, more overhead), or Fernet (symmetric encryption, overkill for cursors). Recommendation: itsdangerous.

meta.smartest_note: Escalated to Smartest -- encryption library comparison benefits from Claude's training on security best practices across ecosystems.

steps.text: Agreed on itsdangerous. The cursor interface won't change -- just the encoding layer. I'll swap base64 for HMAC-signed tokens. Existing tests still pass because they use the cursor value opaquely.

steps.title: Resolution -- team converges
steps.explanation: The security issue is resolved collaboratively. All four agents contributed at exactly the right moments.

scores.reason: Owns the fix. Implementation lead again.
Research delivered. Stepping back naturally.
Needs to verify the fix.
Monitoring but not driving.

steps.insight: Compare this to the Performance branch: there, the Researcher NEVER re-engages. Same four agents, completely different conversation because of what the tester found.

phases.name: DEEP EXECUTE
phases.description: A performance problem keeps the conversation in implementation mode.

steps.text: Performance problem. Watch how the Developer STAYS dominant -- no topic shift means the Researcher stays dormant.

Performance cliff at scale: pagination works fine through page 400, then response times spike from 45ms to 12,000ms. The cursor-based query is doing a full table scan when the offset exceeds the index buffer. We need to optimize the query implementation.

steps.title: No topic shift -- Developer stays hot
steps.explanation: 'optimize', 'implementation', 'query' -- these are EXECUTE-phase keywords. The Researcher's triggers ('research', 'investigate') are NOT present. No re-engagement.

scores.reason: 'optimize', 'query', 'implementation' -- all Developer triggers. Novelty restored by new problem.
Owns the finding. Performance testing expertise.
Index strategy is architectural. Moderate relevance.
STILL DORMANT. No research/investigation keywords. Threshold 1.00 remains.

steps.insight: The Researcher stays at 0.18 DORMANT. In the Security branch, they're at 0.61 ACTIVE right now. Same agent, same conversation history up to this point -- the only difference is what the tester found.
steps.cost_note: Researcher still gated. Without Cohort, they'd generate a ~3.5K token response about 'researching performance optimization patterns' that adds nothing the Developer doesn't already know.

steps.text: Found the issue: keyset pagination, not offset pagination. Instead of WHERE id > cursor_id, the query was using OFFSET which degrades linearly. Switching to keyset: WHERE (sort_col, id) > (cursor_sort_val, cursor_id) with a composite index.

meta.smartest_note: Escalated to Smartest -- keyset vs offset pagination trade-offs benefit from Claude's deep understanding of database query optimization.

steps.text: Good fix. But this changes the cursor contract -- it now needs to encode both the sort column value AND the row ID. Make sure the cursor token format is versioned so we can evolve it.
Already handled. The cursor is a signed JSON blob: {v: 1, sort_val: ..., id: ..., dir: 'asc'}. Version field means we can change the format without breaking existing cursors.

steps.title: Developer-Architect loop resolves the issue
steps.explanation: This was a technical problem with a technical solution. The right two agents handled it. The Researcher and Tester stayed appropriately quiet.

scores.reason: Resolved the issue. Novelty dropping again after 2 messages.
Design review contribution. Appropriate involvement.
Needs to re-run performance tests. Relevant.
Never re-engaged. This was the right outcome -- no research was needed.

steps.insight: In the Security branch, all 4 agents contributed. Here, only 3 did meaningful work. Cohort correctly determined the Researcher had nothing to add to a performance optimization problem.

compat+security.title: Conservative & Secure
compat+security.summary: Your team built a backward-compatible pagination layer with HMAC-signed cursor tokens. All 4 agents contributed meaningfully.
compat+security.key_moment: The Researcher's constraint discovery (14 internal callers, 2 with strict schemas) directly shaped the two-phase migration approach. Without backward-compat choice, this constraint never surfaces.

compat+performance.title: Conservative & Optimized
compat+performance.summary: Your team built a backward-compatible pagination layer with keyset optimization. The Researcher contributed constraints but never re-engaged after going silent.
compat+performance.key_moment: The Developer had to navigate both backward-compatibility constraints AND a performance fix. The hardest path -- but the most realistic for production systems.

clean+security.title: Fast & Secure
clean+security.summary: Your team shipped a clean v2 endpoint with HMAC-signed cursors. The Developer moved fast, then the Researcher re-engaged for the security fix.
clean+security.key_moment: The Researcher went fully DORMANT in the PLAN phase -- no constraints to research. Then snapped back to ACTIVE when the security finding required investigation. Cohort's topic shift detection at its most dramatic.

clean+performance.title: Fast & Lean
clean+performance.summary: Your team shipped a clean v2 endpoint with keyset pagination. Only 3 agents did meaningful work -- the Researcher correctly stayed silent throughout.
clean+performance.key_moment: The Researcher never spoke after the opening phase. This is Cohort working correctly -- not every agent needs to contribute to every conversation. The system saved tokens and time by keeping irrelevant expertise silent.
